SURGICAL PATHOLOGY REPORT
Memorial Regional Medical Center — Department of Pathology
Accession: SP-2025-014782
Specimen received: 2025-09-12
Report finalized: 2025-09-15
Pathologist: M. Alvarez, MD (Board-certified, Anatomic Pathology)

CLINICAL HISTORY
72-year-old former smoker (45 pack-years, quit 2014) with a 3.2 cm right upper
lobe spiculated mass identified on staging CT. Referred for CT-guided core
biopsy by pulmonology service. No prior thoracic malignancy. No known
extrathoracic primary.

SPECIMEN
A. Right upper lobe, CT-guided core biopsy — three cores, aggregate 1.4 cm,
   tan-white, firm.

GROSS DESCRIPTION
The specimen is received in formalin labeled with the patient's name and
"right upper lobe core biopsy." Three linear cores measuring 1.0, 1.2, and
1.4 cm in greatest dimension are submitted entirely in cassette A1.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION
Sections show fragments of lung parenchyma extensively replaced by an
infiltrating glandular neoplasm. Tumor cells are arranged in acinar and
lepidic patterns with focal papillary architecture. Nuclei are enlarged,
vesicular, and show prominent nucleoli. Mitotic activity is brisk
(approximately 12 per 10 high-power fields). No necrosis is identified in
this limited sample. Background lung shows mild emphysematous change.

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY
TTF-1: positive (diffuse, strong)
Napsin A: positive
p40: negative
CK7: positive
CK20: negative
The immunoprofile supports a primary lung adenocarcinoma and excludes
squamous and metastatic colorectal origin.

MOLECULAR (sent out, results pending at sign-out)
EGFR, ALK, ROS1, BRAF, KRAS, PD-L1 — pending.

FINAL DIAGNOSIS
Right upper lobe, core biopsy:
   ADENOCARCINOMA OF THE LUNG, moderately differentiated.
   See microscopic description and immunohistochemistry.

Synoptic summary: NSCLC, adenocarcinoma subtype.

Electronically signed: M. Alvarez, MD — 2025-09-15 16:42
